params.desc: The PPIs to use for the prior network. If a pandas DataFrame is provided, it must have the columns

params.name: input_nodes
params.desc: A dictionary of input nodes. The keys are the node names, the values are the node scores.

params.name: output_nodes
params.desc: A dictionary of output nodes. The keys are the node names, the values are the node scores.

signature: find_causalnet(prior_graph, input_node_scores, output_node_scores, node_weights=None, node_cutoff=0.1, min_penalty=0.01, max_penalty=1.0, missing_penalty=10, edge_penalty=0.01, solver=None, seed=1337, max_runs=1, stable_runs=5, verbose=True, **kwargs)
description: Find the causal network that best explains the input/output node scores.

params.name: prior_graph
params.desc: The prior graph to use for the search.

params.name: input_node_scores
params.desc: A dictionary of input node scores.

params.name: output_node_scores
params.desc: A dictionary of output node scores.

params.name: node_weights
params.desc: A dictionary of node weights. The keys are the node names, the values are the weights. If None, all nodes will have the same weight.

params.name: node_cutoff
params.desc: The cutoff to use for the node weights. Nodes with a weight below this cutoff will be assigned the max_penalty, nodes with a weight above this cutoff will be assigned the min_penalty. Only used if node_weights is not None. Default: 0.1

params.name: min_penalty
params.desc: The minimum penalty to assign to nodes with a weight above the cutoff. Only used if node_weights is not None. Default: 0.01

params.name: max_penalty
params.desc: The maximum penalty to assign to nodes with a weight below the cutoff Only used if node_weights is not None. Default: 1.0

params.name: missing_penalty
params.desc: The penalty to assign to nodes that are not measured. Default: 10

params.name: edge_penalty
params.desc: The penalty to assign to edges. Default: 0.01

params.name: solver
params.desc: The solver to use. If None, the default solver will be used. Default: None It will default to the solver included in SCIPY, if no other solver is available.

params.name: seed
params.desc: The seed to use for the random number generator. Default: 1337

params.name: max_runs
params.desc: The maximum number of runs to perform. Consider increasing this value if the solver does not converge. In each run, the noise added to the edge and node penalties is perturbed slightly (iterating over the seed). By default, only 1 run is performed.

params.name: stable_runs
params.desc: The number of consecutive stable solutions requires to interrupt the iteration over max_runs. Only used if max_runs is not == 1. Default: 5

params.name: verbose
params.desc: Whether to print progress information. Default: True

params.name: **kwargs
params.desc: Additional arguments to pass to the solver.

params.name: return
params.desc: Not specified.

params.name: groupby

params.name: resource_name
params.desc: Name of the resource to be used for ligand-receptor inference. See `li.rs.show_resources()` for available resources.

params.name: expr_prop
params.desc: Minimum expression proportion for the ligands and receptors (+ their subunits) in the corresponding cell identities. Set to 0 to return unfiltered results.

params.name: min_cells

params.name: groupby_pairs

params.desc: Additional columns to be added from any of the methods implemented in liana, or any of the columns returned by `scanpy.tl.rank_genes_groups`, each starting with ligand_* or receptor_*. For example, ['ligand_pvals', 'receptor_pvals']. None by default.

params.name: return_all_lrs

params.desc: Key under which the results will be stored in `adata.uns` if `inplace` is True.

params.name: use_raw
params.desc: Use raw attribute of adata if present.

params.name: layer
params.desc: Layer in anndata.AnnData.layers to use. If None, use anndata.AnnData.X.

params.name: de_method
params.desc: Differential expression method. `scanpy.tl.rank_genes_groups` is used to rank genes according to 1vsRest. The default method is 't-test'.

params.name: verbose
params.desc: Verbosity flag.

params.name: n_perms
params.desc: Number of permutations for the permutation test. Relevant only for permutation-based methods (e.g., CellPhoneDB). If None is passed, no permutation testing is performed.

params.name: seed
params.desc: Random seed for reproducibility.

params.name: n_jobs
params.desc: Number of jobs to run in parallel.

params.name: resource
params.desc: A pandas dataframe with [ligand, receptor] columns. If provided will overrule the resource requested via resource_name

params.name: interactions
params.desc: List of tuples with ligand-receptor pairs [(ligand, receptor), ...] to be used for the analysis. If passed, it will overrule the resource requested via resource and resource_name.

params.name: mdata_kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments to be passed to li.fun.mdata_to_anndata if adata is an instance of MuData. If an AnnData object is passed, these arguments are ignored.

params.name: inplace
params.desc: Whether to store results in place, or else to return them.

params.name: return
params.desc: If inplace = False, returns a DataFrame with ligand-receptor results. Otherwise, modifies the adata object with the following key: anndata.AnnData.uns [key_added] with the aforementioned DataFrame

description: A method for bivariate local spatial metrics.

params.name: mdata
params.desc: MuData (multimodal) data object.

params.name: local_name
params.desc: Name of the local function to use for the analysis. Passing None will return only the Global scores.

params.name: global_name
params.desc: Name or names (list) of the global function(s) to use for the analysis. Passing None will not calculate any global scores

params.name: interactions
params.desc: List of tuples with ligand-receptor pairs [(ligand, receptor), ...] to be used for the analysis. If passed, it will overrule the resource requested via resource and resource_name.

params.name: add_categories

params.desc: Number of permutations for the permutation test. If None, no p-values are computed.

params.name: seed
params.desc: Random seed for reproducibility.

params.name: nz_prop

params.name: complex_sep
params.desc: Separator to use for complex names.

params.name: xy_sep
params.desc: Separator to use for interaction names.

params.name: remove_self_interactions
params.desc: Whether to remove self-interactions. True by default.

params.name: verbose
params.desc: Verbosity flag.

params.name: max_neighs
params.desc: The maximum number of neighbors to consider when constructing the Paraview.

params.name: verbose
params.desc: Whether to print progress.

params.name: return
params.desc: MistyData object with the intra view, and two fixed extra view(s): para and juxta.

signature: spatial_neighbors(adata: anndata._core.anndata.AnnData, bandwidth=None, cutoff=0.1, max_neighbours=100, kernel='gaussian', set_diag=False, zoi=0, standardize=False, reference=None, spatial_key='spatial', key_added='spatial', inplace=True)
description: Generate spatial connectivity weights using Euclidean distance.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: bandwidth
params.desc: Denotes signaling length (l) and controls the maximum distance at which two spots are considered. Corresponds to the units in which spatial coordinates are expressed.

params.name: cutoff
params.desc: Values below this cutoff will be set to 0.

params.name: max_neighbours
params.desc: Maximum nearest neighbours to be considered when generating spatial connectivity weights. Essentially, the maximum number of edges in the spatial connectivity graph.

params.name: kernel
params.desc: Kernel function used to generate connectivity weights. It controls the shape of the connectivity weights. The following options are available: ['gaussian', 'exponential', 'linear', 'misty_rbf']

params.name: set_diag
params.desc: Logical, sets connectivity diagonal to 0 if False. Default is True.

params.name: zoi
params.desc: Zone of indifference. Values below this cutoff will be set to np.inf.

params.name: standardize
params.desc: Whether to (l1) standardize spatial proximities (connectivities) so that they sum to 1. This plays a role when weighing border regions prior to downstream methods, as the number of spots in the border region (and hence the sum of proximities) is smaller than the number of spots in the center. Relevant for methods with unstandardized scores (e.g. product). Default is False.

params.name: reference
params.desc: Reference coordinates to use when generating spatial connectivity weights. If None, uses the spatial coordinates in adata.obsm[spatial_key]. This is only relevant if you want to use a different set of coordinates to generate spatial connectivity weights.

params.name: spatial_key
params.desc: Key in adata.obsm that contains the spatial coordinates. Default is 'spatial'.

params.name: key_added
params.desc: Key to add to adata.obsp if inplace = True. If reference is not None, key will be added to adata.obsm.

params.name: inplace
params.desc: Whether to store results in place, or else to return them.

params.name: return

params.name: verbose

params.name: n_perms
params.desc: Number of permutations for the permutation test. Relevant only for permutation-based methods (e.g., CellPhoneDB). If None is passed, no permutation testing is performed.

params.name: n_jobs
params.desc: Number of jobs to run in parallel.

params.name: interactions
params.desc: List of tuples with ligand-receptor pairs [(ligand, receptor), ...] to be used for the analysis. If passed, it will overrule the resource requested via resource and resource_name.

params.name: mdata_kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments to be passed to li.fun.mdata_to_anndata if adata is an instance of MuData. If an AnnData object is passed, these arguments are ignored.

params.name: inplace
params.desc: Whether to store results in place, or else to return them.

params.name: return
params.desc: If inplace = False, returns a DataFrame with ligand-receptor results Otherwise, modifies the adata object with the following key: anndata.AnnData.uns [key_added] with the aforementioned DataFrame
